interpretation: Preserves oracle-frontier score within a narrow interval while reducing all-depth evaluation effort.
Validation-calibrated gate keeps score near the all-depth frontier while reducing planning time.
Seed-stable skip gate preserves sparse-frontier score and reduces sparse-frontier evaluation time.

contrast: diverse top512 vs budget32 score; exact forms vs wide128
interpretation: Policy shortlist improves the small-budget score while using far fewer exact evaluations than wide128.

contrast: learned prior vs same-budget random-prior mean
interpretation: Score effect is small and runtime-positive, so this remains a limited quality signal, not a speed claim.

contrast: learned prior vs no-prior under top-8/top-12 budgets
interpretation: Low-budget learned prior has a bounded quality effect with visible runtime cost.

contrast: neural guard vs deterministic Boolean guard
interpretation: Quality effect is small and runtime-positive, so this remains a limited guard diagnostic.

contrast: heuristic top-4 plus neural top-12 vs beam4
interpretation: Root-only candidate extension is bounded score evidence; runtime is not claimed.
